title: Two competing AgentSpec shapes: TypeScript type vs YAML input
description: The TypeScript AgentSpec type defines systemPrompt/taskPrompt as top-level fields. The YAML parser (parseAgentYaml) reads prompt.system/prompt.task. These are two different shapes for the same concept. An author writing to the TypeScript shape produces YAML with top-level systemPrompt/taskPrompt keys — which parseAgentYaml ignores because it reads from the prompt.* namespace. The parser's output shape differs from the YAML input shape.
actual_behavior: Writing { systemPrompt: '...', taskPrompt: '...' } to author-agent-spec produces YAML with top-level systemPrompt/taskPrompt keys. parseAgentYaml reads spec.prompt?.system and spec.prompt?.task — both undefined. No prompt content is produced.
expected_behavior: A single canonical shape for both the TypeScript type and the YAML input. If the TypeScript type is the truth, parseAgentYaml should read top-level fields. If the YAML input is the truth, the TypeScript type should reflect the nested shape.
reproduction: 1. author-agent-spec with { systemPrompt: 'test', taskPrompt: 'test' }. 2. call-agent. 3. Observe compile failure: no prompt content.
workaround: Use prompt.system and prompt.task in the spec object.
